identify_breaking_api.expected_solution: Agent must identify the breaking change in producer `google/cel-go` introduced by (none). The IMPACT_REPORT.md must name the legacy symbols (cel.Program) and their replacements ((none)), and point to producer-side artifacts ((none)).
evaluation_criteria: Names the producer repository `google/cel-go` and the breaking PR/commit ((none))
References at least one producer-side file (basename: (none))

find_direct_consumers.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate every consumer file in grpc/grpc-go that uses the deprecated symbols (cel.Program). Per ground_truth: grpc-go: security/authorization/engine/engine_test.go, security/authorization/go.mod, security/authorization/go.sum. The report must cite each consumer file by name and indicate which deprecated symbol it depends on.
evaluation_criteria: Cites each consumer file by name (basenames: engine_test.go, go.mod, and go.sum)
For each cited consumer, names which deprecated symbol from cel.Program it uses
Distinguishes files with compile-time dependencies from files that only forward/proxy the symbol

trace_transitive_impact.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive impact: which downstream code paths in grpc/grpc-go indirectly observe the producer change. The answer must identify forwarding/middleware/interceptor code that propagates the affected types and explain why the type or contract change cascades beyond the directly-importing files. Reference the consumer fix PR(s) (grpc/grpc-go#5243) where applicable.
evaluation_criteria: Identifies at least one transitive code path beyond the direct importers (interceptor/middleware/wrapper)
Explains the propagation mechanism (type changes, signature changes, behavior drift) for cel.Program
Cross-references the consumer-side fix PR(s) (grpc/grpc-go#5243) or the migration pattern they encode

classify_breakage.expected_solution: Agent must classify each affected file by failure mode. The ground-truth breakage type is `compile_error`. For `compile_and_runtime` cases the report must identify both compile-time breakage (undefined functions, signature mismatches) and runtime behavior changes (silent metadata loss, semantic shifts). For `compile_only` or `runtime_only` cases, justify why only one class applies.
evaluation_criteria: Distinguishes compile-time failures (undefined functions, type errors) from runtime behavior changes (silent semantics drift)
Identifies at least one specific consumer file per failure class consistent with the classification
